UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents the application user, typically a landlord or property manager, who manages properties within the system. This entity stores metadata about the user, not authentication details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The full name of the user.

email.description: The primary email address of the user.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Property.title: Property
Property.description: Details about a rental property managed by a user.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Property entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who owns or manages this property. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Property)

addressLine1.description: The first line of the property's address.

addressLine2.description: The second line of the property's address (optional).

city.description: The city where the property is located.

propertyType.description: The type of property. (e.g., Flat, House, Bungalow, Maisonette, Studio, HMO)

status.description: The current occupancy status of the property. (e.g., Vacant, Occupied, Under Maintenance)

numberOfBedrooms.description: The number of bedrooms in the property.

numberOfBathrooms.description: The number of bathrooms in the property.

mainImageUrl.description: URL to the primary image of the property.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the property record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the property record was last updated.

Tenant.title: Tenant
Tenant.description: Information about a tenant renting a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tenant entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who manages this tenant. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Tenant)

name.description: The full name of the tenant.

email.description: The email address of the tenant.

phoneNumber.description: The telephone number of the tenant.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the tenant record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the tenant record was last updated.

Tenancy.title: Tenancy
Tenancy.description: Represents a specific rental agreement between a property and a tenant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tenancy entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who manages this tenancy. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Tenancy)

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property associated with this tenancy. (Relationship: Property 1:N Tenancy)

tenantId.description: Reference to the primary Tenant associated with this tenancy. (Relationship: Tenant 1:N Tenancy)

startDate.description: The start date of the tenancy agreement.

endDate.description: The end date of the tenancy agreement. Null if open-ended.

notes.description: General notes related to the tenancy agreement.

depositAmount.description: The security deposit amount paid for this tenancy.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the tenancy record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the tenancy record was last updated.

MaintenanceRequest.title: MaintenanceRequest
MaintenanceRequest.description: Records details of a maintenance issue reported for a property.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MaintenanceRequest entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who manages this request. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MaintenanceRequest)

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property where the maintenance issue occurred. (Relationship: Property 1:N MaintenanceRequest)

tenantId.description: Reference to the Tenant who reported the issue, if applicable. (Relationship: Tenant 1:N MaintenanceRequest)

description.description: A detailed description of the maintenance issue.

urgency.description: The urgency level of the request. (e.g., Low, Medium, High, Critical)

status.description: The current status of the maintenance request. (e.g., Reported, In Progress, Awaiting Parts, Completed, Cancelled)

reportedDate.description: The date and time when the maintenance issue was reported.

scheduledDate.description: The date and time when maintenance work is scheduled, if applicable.

resolutionDate.description: The date and time when the maintenance issue was resolved.

internalNotes.description: Internal notes or updates on the maintenance process.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance request record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the maintenance request record was last updated.

Inspection.title: Inspection
Inspection.description: Schedules and records details of property inspections.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Inspection entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who scheduled this inspection. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Inspection)

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property being inspected. (Relationship: Property 1:N Inspection)

inspectorName.description: The name of the person or company conducting the inspection.

scheduledDate.description: The planned date and time for the inspection.

completedDate.description: The actual date and time when the inspection was completed.

type.description: The type of inspection. (e.g., Move-in, Mid-tenancy, Move-out, Routine)

notes.description: Detailed notes and findings from the inspection.

status.description: The current status of the inspection. (e.g., Scheduled, Completed, Cancelled)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the inspection record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the inspection record was last updated.

Document.title: Document
Document.description: Stores metadata and URLs for various documents related to properties, tenancies, or maintenance.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Document entity.

userProfileId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who uploaded this document. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Document)

propertyId.description: Reference to the Property this document is associated with. (Relationship: Property 1:N Document)

tenancyId.description: Reference to the Tenancy this document is associated with, if applicable. (Relationship: Tenancy 1:N Document)

maintenanceRequestId.description: Reference to the MaintenanceRequest this document is associated with, if applicable (e.g., invoice, quote). (Relationship: MaintenanceRequest 1:N Document)

title.description: The title or name of the document.

documentType.description: The type of document. (e.g., Tenancy Agreement, EPC, Gas Safety Certificate, Invoice, Photo, Lease Agreement)

url.description: The URL where the document file is stored.

uploadDate.description: The date and time when the document was uploaded.

description.description: An optional description or summary of the document content.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the document record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. The 'id' field in the document MUST match the 'userProfileId' path parameter and the authenticated user's UID (request.auth.uid).

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user profile, which corresponds to the Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Collection of properties managed by a specific user. Each document includes 'userProfileId' for authorization independence, matching the parent 'userProfileId' parameter.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user profile who owns this property.

params.name: propertyId
params.description: The unique ID of the property.

definition.description: Collection of tenants associated with a specific user. Each document includes 'userProfileId' for authorization independence, matching the parent 'userProfileId' parameter.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user profile who manages this tenant.

params.name: tenantId
params.description: The unique ID of the tenant.

definition.description: Collection of tenancy agreements managed by a specific user. Each document includes 'userProfileId' for authorization independence, matching the parent 'userProfileId' parameter. Also includes 'propertyId' and 'tenantId' as foreign keys.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user profile who manages this tenancy.

params.name: tenancyId
params.description: The unique ID of the tenancy agreement.

definition.description: Collection of maintenance requests associated with a specific user. Each document includes 'userProfileId' for authorization independence, matching the parent 'userProfileId' parameter. Also includes 'propertyId' and 'tenantId' as foreign keys.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user profile who manages this maintenance request.

params.name: maintenanceRequestId
params.description: The unique ID of the maintenance request.

definition.description: Collection of property inspections scheduled or recorded by a specific user. Each document includes 'userProfileId' for authorization independence, matching the parent 'userProfileId' parameter. Also includes 'propertyId' as a foreign key.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user profile who manages this inspection.

params.name: inspectionId
params.description: The unique ID of the inspection.

definition.description: Collection of documents uploaded by a specific user. Each document includes 'userProfileId' for authorization independence, matching the parent 'userProfileId' parameter. May also include 'propertyId', 'tenancyId', and 'maintenanceRequestId' as foreign keys.

params.name: userProfileId
params.description: The unique ID of the user profile who uploaded this document.

params.name: documentId
params.description: The unique ID of the document.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes strong ownership, authorization independence, and query efficiency by nesting all user-specific data under a dedicated user profile path. Each authenticated user (identified by their `request.auth.uid`) will have their `UserProfile` stored at `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}`, where `userProfileId` explicitly matches their `uid`. All subsequent data entities they own or manage – `Properties`, `Tenants`, `Tenancies`, `MaintenanceRequests`, `Inspections`, and `Documents` – will reside as subcollections under their respective user profile path (e.g., `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/properties/{propertyId}`).

This structure achieves **Authorization Independence** by ensuring that every document related to a user (Property, Tenant, Tenancy, etc.) includes a `userProfileId` field. This field's value will always match the `userProfileId` wildcard in its path, and critically, will also match `request.auth.uid` for the authenticated user. This allows security rules to evaluate permissions solely based on the document's own fields and the request's authentication context, eliminating the need for `get()` operations on parent documents. This simplifies rule writing, makes rules robust against concurrent modifications, and enhances transactional integrity.

To support **Query Accurately and Performantly (QAPs)**, the structural segregation is key. The client-side application will query specific subcollections under the authenticated user's profile, such as `/userProfiles/{request.auth.uid}/tenants`. Firestore Security Rules can then easily and efficiently enforce that `list` (and `get`, `create`, `update`, `delete`) operations are only permitted if `request.auth.uid` matches the `{userProfileId}` in the path. This guarantees that a user can only list or access their own data, preventing wide, unfiltered queries (which security rules cannot efficiently filter anyway) and ensuring that the security rules act as a gatekeeper, not a filter. The original `Runtime FirebaseError` listing from a top-level `tenants` collection is directly addressed by this hierarchical, user-scoped structure, as client queries will now correctly target `/userProfiles/{userProfileId}/tenants` to access user-specific tenant data.
